/matchups/falco/gameplan.org:
* Laser 
** Options After Parry
I don't deliberately think about these options constantly throughout a match as I feel you'd get too predictable if you literally only mixup between them but when playing intuitively isn't working I will be more deliberate about trying to figure out whats beating me and pick options accordingly.

*** sh back fair
I realized watching all these that this is definitely my most used option lol. Its pretty low risk/high reward.

Really good falcos can beat it though with big overshoots -> turnaround utilt and other stuff prolly.

Nice to throw out when need to regain footing and want something solid

#+BEGIN_SRC pusdb-cmd youtube oxIcA8qnD-A
#+END_SRC

*** wavedash forward dtilt
good when they are ~2 falco full momentum sh's away

#+BEGIN_SRC pusdb-cmd youtube 1evHKYhEBW0
#+END_SRC

*** sh back -> djc forward nair/uair
good when they are trying to laser on top of you and can't cc nair

can still be ok below cc to get some chip damage but gotta be careful when they are good out of cc

#+BEGIN_SRC pusdb-cmd youtube vCeXy_jM9Rw
#+END_SRC

*** sh forward -> djc nair/uair
good when they are close but just out of range of being able to shine you when they land

sometimes just gotta hope you get the laser ps and risk it for the biscuit

good to catch them shorthopping after the laser

assume they will shield after the laser and try to land behind them oustide of shine oos range. Can then go into "Exploit being behind shield" mode

#+BEGIN_SRC pusdb-cmd youtube OznnheaPvGU
#+END_SRC

*** sh/fh drift (usually fair)
good for when you are in a laser stalement. For gathering information/repositioning/even just forcing an unexpected interaction for them

#+BEGIN_SRC pusdb-cmd youtube qdVz_os46Zg
#+END_SRC

*** wd down
also good for when you are in a laser stalement but a less commital/more defensive option

** take laser > parry
I'm not very good about setting these up deliberately but super good whenever they happen. My biggest takeaway from his excercise is I want to try to use take laser options more often.

#+BEGIN_SRC pusdb-cmd youtube 9B6bxTNiBGw
#+END_SRC

** empty (wave)land
A good way to set up laser parry. if I get stunlocked from a distance I usually like to take a beat (just let the next laser hit me) and then sh time -> time fastfall to land and parry the laser

#+BEGIN_SRC pusdb-cmd youtube SJzex2OuU3s
#+END_SRC

* Other concepets
** ledgedash down
good way to get off the ledge

doesnt need to be super clean

#+END_SRC

** Parry sh fair
throw in parry sh in place fair on level 1 dair/nairs when they below 30% especially

#+BEGIN_SRC pusdb-cmd youtube oMKBbVdrJNU
#+END_SRC

** Exploit being behind shield
They basically always sh bair backwards

#+BEGIN_SRC pusdb-cmd youtube o4pWKeyBpT4
#+END_SRC

** offbeat play
try to internalize the timing difference between falco doing immediate dash forward dair and 1 dashback/dashforward dair. This is one beat. Look for opportunities where you can get to positions one beat sooner/later than they expect.

Wavedash/land back can be a good way to do this because even if they are attacking that spot, yoshis tail being lower than head can sometimes be just enough of a beat go get out parry.

#+BEGIN_SRC pusdb-cmd youtube lBAMl2fWsMA
#+END_SRC

I don't think these are the best example clips tbh, will update as I find more

*** dash back -> sh dj turn -> waveland down
This is a specific offbeat setup that I use a lot. 

A good low commital way to bait out interactions while holding your ground.

#+BEGIN_SRC pusdb-cmd youtube IinrNMvYXFc
#+END_SRC

** w/ no clips yet
try to parry djc uair instead of nair when you get the level 1 dair/nairs and they are above ~30%

djc no fastfall nair can be good for catching jumps and oftentimes better when you realize too late they are gonna shield camp cuz can turn an easy sh oos into landing behind them
